heroText: Yaren is a capital city in Nauru, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.label: Makwa
history.text: Yaren was formerly known as Makwa or Moqua, names connected with Moqua Well in the district.
Yaren District was created in 1968, the same year Nauru became an independent republic.

history.label: Government
history.text: Yaren became the location of Nauru's parliament, government offices and other national administration buildings.

history.label: Airport
history.text: Nauru International Airport is located in Yaren, giving the district its main transport role.

history.label: Constituency
history.text: Yaren is also a political constituency returning two members to the Parliament of Nauru.

history.label: Today
history.text: Modern Yaren functions as Nauru's de facto capital district, though the country has no official capital city.
